Mobile Banking: Text Analysis from customers’ reviews on iOS and Google Store

Chi Dao

library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(lubridate)
library(tidytext)
library(stopwords)
library(cld3)
library(wordcloud2)

DATA_RAW <- "data_raw"

1. Import data

Make sure all the data files are in the working directory

csv_paths <- list.files(DATA_RAW, pattern = "\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)
cat("CSV files found:\n"); print(basename(csv_paths))
## CSV files found:
## [1] "bank_of_america__combined_us.csv"        
## [2] "chase__combined_us.csv"                  
## [3] "citi__combined_us.csv"                   
## [4] "marcus_by_goldman_sachs__combined_us.csv"
## [5] "wells_fargo__combined_us.csv"
if (length(csv_paths) == 0) {
  stop("No CSVs found in data_raw/. Move your combined CSVs into: ", file.path(getwd(), DATA_RAW))
}

# import files
raw <- purrr::map_dfr(csv_paths, function(p) {
  readr::read_csv(
    p,
    show_col_types = FALSE,
    col_types = readr::cols(.default = readr::col_character())
  ) %>%
    mutate(source_file = basename(p))
})

2. Data Overview

glimpse(raw)
## Rows: 5,450
## Columns: 14
## $ platform      <chr> "app_store", "app_store", "app_store", "app_store", "app…

## $ app_id        <chr> "284847138.0", "284847138.0", "284847138.0", "284847138.…

## $ date          <chr> "2025-12-28T13:28:49-07:00", "2025-12-28T13:28:13-07:00"…

## $ rating        <chr> "1", "5", "5", "5", "5", "1", "1", "1", "5", "1", "5", "…
## $ title         <chr> "Horrible.", "Enjoy", "Bank Of America Application Benef…
## $ review        <chr> "I can’t load the banking app bc it requires a different…
## $ version       <chr> "25.04.06", "25.12.0", "25.12.0", "25.12.0", "25.12.0", …

## $ thumbsUpCount <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, …

## $ source_file   <chr> "bank_of_america__combined_us.csv", "bank_of_america__co…
summary (raw)
##    platform          storefront           app_id           review_id        
##  Length:5450        Length:5450        Length:5450        Length:5450       
##  Class :character   Class :character   Class :character   Class :character  
##  Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Mode  :character  
##      date               user              rating             title          
##  Length:5450        Length:5450        Length:5450        Length:5450

##  Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Mode  :character  
##     review            version            package          thumbsUpCount

##  Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Mode  :character  
##   appVersion        source_file       
##  Length:5450        Length:5450       
##  Class :character   Class :character  
##  Mode  :character   Mode  :character
str(raw)
## tibble [5,450 × 14] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame)
##  $ platform     : chr [1:5450] "app_store" "app_store" "app_store" "app_store" ...
##  $ storefront   : chr [1:5450] "us" "us" "us" "us" ...
##  $ app_id       : chr [1:5450] "284847138.0" "284847138.0" "284847138.0" "284847138.0" ...
##  $ review_id    : chr [1:5450] "13566342702" "13566341104" "13566049201" "13565792340" ...
##  $ date         : chr [1:5450] "2025-12-28T13:28:49-07:00" "2025-12-28T13:28:13-07:00" "2025-12-28T11:48:52-07:00" "2025-12-28T10:27:09-07:00" ...
##  $ user         : chr [1:5450] "EBP1940" "papemahi" "Jiankun Li" "Tom nrady" ...
##  $ rating       : chr [1:5450] "1" "5" "5" "5" ...
##  $ title        : chr [1:5450] "Horrible." "Enjoy" "Bank Of America Application Benefit" "App" ...
##  $ review       : chr [1:5450] "I can’t load the banking app bc it requires a different apple phone now.  Horrible.  Customer service is sleepi"| __truncated__ "Good\nGood" "Bank Of America has an application which allows you to track all your spendings, which is a good method to know"| __truncated__ "The app is easy to use for a 63-year-old woman and I like the Bank Of America and Watertown Massachusetts that "| __truncated__ ...
##  $ version      : chr [1:5450] "25.04.06" "25.12.0" "25.12.0" "25.12.0" ...
##  $ package      : chr [1:5450] NA NA NA NA ...
##  $ thumbsUpCount: chr [1:5450] NA NA NA NA ...
##  $ appVersion   : chr [1:5450] NA NA NA NA ...
##  $ source_file  : chr [1:5450] "bank_of_america__combined_us.csv" "bank_of_america__combined_us.csv" "bank_of_america__combined_us.csv" "bank_of_america__combined_us.csv" ...
raw %>%
  count(platform, name = "n_reviews")
## # A tibble: 2 × 2
##   platform    n_reviews
##   <chr>           <int>
## 1 app_store        2450
## 2 google_play      3000
missing_raw <- raw %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "n_missing"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(n_missing))

missing_raw
## # A tibble: 14 × 2
##    variable      n_missing
##    <chr>             <int>
##  1 app_id             3000
##  2 title              3000
##  3 version            3000
##  4 appVersion         2733
##  5 package            2450
##  6 thumbsUpCount      2450
##  7 platform              0
##  8 storefront            0
##  9 review_id             0
## 10 date                  0
## 11 user                  0
## 12 rating                0
## 13 review                0
## 14 source_file           0
raw %>%
  group_by(platform) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(is.na(.))))
## # A tibble: 2 × 14
##   platform   storefront app_id review_id  date  user rating title review version
##   <chr>           <int>  <int>     <int> <int> <int>  <int> <int>  <int>   <int>
## 1 app_store           0      0         0     0     0      0     0      0       0
## 2 google_pl…          0   3000         0     0     0      0  3000      0    3000
## # ℹ 4 more variables: package <int>, thumbsUpCount <int>, appVersion <int>,
## #   source_file <int>
raw <- raw %>%
  mutate(
    raw_text = paste(coalesce(title, ""), coalesce(review, "")),
    raw_length_words = stringr::str_count(raw_text, "\\S+")
  )

summary(raw$raw_length_words)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##    1.00    4.00   12.00   22.93   30.00  411.00
# rating by platform
ggplot(raw, aes(rating)) +
  geom_bar() +
  facet_wrap(~ platform) +
  labs(
    title = "Rating distribution by platform (raw data)",
    x = "Star rating",
    y = "Number of reviews"
  )

3. Data Preprocessing

# standardize platform name and get bank name from files' name
df1 <- raw %>%
  mutate(
    bank = source_file %>%
      str_replace("__combined_us\\.csv$", "") %>%
      str_replace_all("_", " ") %>%
      str_to_title(),
    platform = case_when(
      platform == "app_store" ~ "iOS",
      platform == "google_play" ~ "Android",
      TRUE ~ as.character(platform)
    )
  )

df1 %>% count(bank, platform)
## # A tibble: 10 × 3
##    bank                    platform     n
##    <chr>                   <chr>    <int>
##  1 Bank Of America         Android    600
##  2 Bank Of America         iOS        490
##  3 Chase                   Android    600
##  4 Chase                   iOS        490
##  5 Citi                    Android    600
##  6 Citi                    iOS        490
##  7 Marcus By Goldman Sachs Android    600
##  8 Marcus By Goldman Sachs iOS        490
##  9 Wells Fargo             Android    600
## 10 Wells Fargo             iOS        490
df2 <- df1 %>%
  mutate(
    rating = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(rating)),
    date = suppressWarnings(ymd_hms(date, quiet = TRUE))
  )

summary(df2$rating)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##   1.000   1.000   5.000   3.559   5.000   5.000
summary(df2$date)
##                  Min.               1st Qu.                Median 
## "2024-01-22 12:28:53" "2025-11-07 18:11:41" "2025-12-11 22:23:04" 
##                  Mean               3rd Qu.                  Max. 
## "2025-09-10 04:50:45" "2025-12-22 17:14:29" "2025-12-29 14:54:15"
head(df2)
## # A tibble: 6 × 17
##   platform storefront app_id    review_id date                user  rating title
##   <chr>    <chr>      <chr>     <chr>     <dttm>              <chr>  <dbl> <chr>
## 1 iOS      us         28484713… 13566342… 2025-12-28 20:28:49 EBP1…      1 Horr…
## 2 iOS      us         28484713… 13566341… 2025-12-28 20:28:13 pape…      5 Enjoy
## 3 iOS      us         28484713… 13566049… 2025-12-28 18:48:52 Jian…      5 Bank…
## 4 iOS      us         28484713… 13565792… 2025-12-28 17:27:09 Tom …      5 App  
## 5 iOS      us         28484713… 13564879… 2025-12-28 12:55:07 Banc…      5 Mi b…
## 6 iOS      us         28484713… 13563526… 2025-12-28 03:53:27 Davi…      1 Whit…
## # ℹ 9 more variables: review <chr>, version <chr>, package <chr>,
## #   thumbsUpCount <chr>, appVersion <chr>, source_file <chr>, raw_text <chr>,
## #   raw_length_words <int>, bank <chr>

Upon checking raw text after combine title and reviews bodies, there are some reviews in different language than English, therefore, it will be removed.

df_lang <- df2 %>%
    mutate(
        language = cld3::detect_language(raw_text)
    )

df_lang %>% count(language, sort = TRUE)
## # A tibble: 59 × 2
##    language     n
##    <chr>    <int>
##  1 en        4213
##  2 <NA>       644
##  3 es         174
##  4 fr          69
##  5 fy          38
##  6 ja          27
##  7 no          26
##  8 nl          25
##  9 gl          18
## 10 sr          16
## # ℹ 49 more rows
df_english <- df_lang %>%
    filter(language == "en")

tibble(
    total_reviews = nrow(df2),
    english_reviews = nrow(df_english),
    removed_non_english = nrow(df2) - nrow(df_english),
    pct_removed = 100 * (nrow(df2) - nrow(df_english)) / nrow(df2)
)
## # A tibble: 1 × 4
##   total_reviews english_reviews removed_non_english pct_removed
##           <int>           <int>               <int>       <dbl>
## 1          5450            4213                1237        22.7

There are 4213 English reviews left.

# create a custom stopword list
custom_stop_words <- bind_rows(
  tibble(word = c("app","bank","banking","mobile", "iphone", "apple", "android", "phone"), 
         lexicon = "custom"),
  tidytext::stop_words
)
reviews_before_sw <- df_english %>%
  unnest_tokens(word, raw_text)

reviews_after_sw <- reviews_before_sw %>%
  anti_join(custom_stop_words, by = "word")

# how many tokens removed
tibble(
  tokens_before = nrow(reviews_before_sw),
  tokens_after = nrow(reviews_after_sw),
  tokens_removed = nrow(reviews_before_sw) - nrow(reviews_after_sw),
  pct_removed = 100 * (nrow(reviews_before_sw) - nrow(reviews_after_sw)) / nrow(reviews_before_sw)
)
## # A tibble: 1 × 4
##   tokens_before tokens_after tokens_removed pct_removed
##           <int>        <int>          <int>       <dbl>
## 1        118393        41956          76437        64.6
# visualize the stopwords that gor removed
removed_words <- reviews_before_sw %>%
    anti_join(reviews_after_sw, by = c("review_id", "word")) %>%
    semi_join(custom_stop_words, by = "word") %>% 
    count(word, sort = TRUE)

wordcloud2(
    data = removed_words,
    size = 0.6,
    shape = "triangle-forward"
)

4. Unigram Sentiment Analysis

BING

bing <- get_sentiments("bing")

bing_words <- reviews_after_sw %>%
  inner_join(bing, by = "word")

head(bing_words)
## # A tibble: 6 × 19
##   platform storefront app_id    review_id date                user  rating title
##   <chr>    <chr>      <chr>     <chr>     <dttm>              <chr>  <dbl> <chr>
## 1 iOS      us         28484713… 13566342… 2025-12-28 20:28:49 EBP1…      1 Horr…
## 2 iOS      us         28484713… 13566342… 2025-12-28 20:28:49 EBP1…      1 Horr…
## 3 iOS      us         28484713… 13566049… 2025-12-28 18:48:52 Jian…      5 Bank…
## 4 iOS      us         28484713… 13565792… 2025-12-28 17:27:09 Tom …      5 App  
## 5 iOS      us         28484713… 13565792… 2025-12-28 17:27:09 Tom …      5 App  
## 6 iOS      us         28484713… 13565792… 2025-12-28 17:27:09 Tom …      5 App  
## # ℹ 11 more variables: review <chr>, version <chr>, package <chr>,
## #   thumbsUpCount <chr>, appVersion <chr>, source_file <chr>,
## #   raw_length_words <int>, bank <chr>, language <chr>, word <chr>,
## #   sentiment <chr>
bing_words %>% count(sentiment)
## # A tibble: 2 × 2
##   sentiment     n
##   <chr>     <int>
## 1 negative   3631
## 2 positive   4370
# overall distribution of sentiment 
bing_words %>%
  count(sentiment) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = sentiment, y = n, fill = sentiment)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Bing sentiment words",
    x = "Sentiment",
    y = "Word count"
  )

# top sentiment words
top_bing_words <- bing_words %>%
  count(sentiment, word, sort = TRUE) %>%
  group_by(sentiment) %>%
  slice_max(n, n = 10) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(top_bing_words,
       aes(x = reorder(word, n), y = n, fill = sentiment)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  coord_flip() +
  facet_wrap(~ sentiment, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Top 10 Bing sentiment words",
    x = "",
    y = "Frequency"
  )

# sentiment words by banks
bing_by_bank <- bing_words %>%
  count(bank, sentiment, word, sort = TRUE) %>%
  group_by(bank, sentiment) %>%
  slice_max(n, n = 10) %>%
  ungroup()

bing_by_bank
## # A tibble: 104 × 4
##    bank            sentiment word            n
##    <chr>           <chr>     <chr>       <int>
##  1 Bank Of America negative  worst          37
##  2 Bank Of America negative  issue          32
##  3 Bank Of America negative  issues         27
##  4 Bank Of America negative  bad            24
##  5 Bank Of America negative  terrible       21
##  6 Bank Of America negative  hard           17
##  7 Bank Of America negative  unable         17
##  8 Bank Of America negative  horrible       15
##  9 Bank Of America negative  frustrating    14
## 10 Bank Of America negative  slow           14
## # ℹ 94 more rows
ggplot(bing_by_bank,
       aes(x = reorder(word, n), y = n, fill = sentiment)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  coord_flip() +
  facet_grid(bank ~ sentiment, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Top Bing sentiment words by bank",
    x = "",
    y = "Frequency"
  )

# sentiment word by platform
bing_by_platform <- bing_words %>%
  count(platform, sentiment, word, sort = TRUE) %>%
  group_by(platform, sentiment) %>%
  slice_max(n, n = 10) %>%
  ungroup()

bing_by_platform
## # A tibble: 41 × 4
##    platform sentiment word            n
##    <chr>    <chr>     <chr>       <int>
##  1 Android  negative  issues         49
##  2 Android  negative  issue          34
##  3 Android  negative  crashes        31
##  4 Android  negative  crashing       26
##  5 Android  negative  worst          23
##  6 Android  negative  hard           18
##  7 Android  negative  frustrating    17
##  8 Android  negative  annoying       15
##  9 Android  negative  bad            15
## 10 Android  negative  slow           15
## # ℹ 31 more rows
ggplot(bing_by_platform,
       aes(x = reorder(word, n), y = n, fill = sentiment)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  coord_flip() +
  facet_grid(platform ~ sentiment, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Top Bing sentiment words by platform",
    x = "",
    y = "Frequency"
  )

bing_words %>%
    count(bank, platform, sentiment) %>%
    group_by(bank, platform) %>%
    mutate(pct = n/sum(n)) 
## # A tibble: 20 × 5
## # Groups:   bank, platform [10]
##    bank                    platform sentiment     n    pct
##    <chr>                   <chr>    <chr>     <int>  <dbl>
##  1 Bank Of America         Android  negative    138 0.234 
##  2 Bank Of America         Android  positive    452 0.766 
##  3 Bank Of America         iOS      negative    592 0.600 
##  4 Bank Of America         iOS      positive    394 0.400 
##  5 Chase                   Android  negative    247 0.490 
##  6 Chase                   Android  positive    257 0.510 
##  7 Chase                   iOS      negative    471 0.527 
##  8 Chase                   iOS      positive    422 0.473 
##  9 Citi                    Android  negative     60 0.0985
## 10 Citi                    Android  positive    549 0.901 
## 11 Citi                    iOS      negative    841 0.715 
## 12 Citi                    iOS      positive    335 0.285 
## 13 Marcus By Goldman Sachs Android  negative    300 0.372 
## 14 Marcus By Goldman Sachs Android  positive    506 0.628 
## 15 Marcus By Goldman Sachs iOS      negative    555 0.424 
## 16 Marcus By Goldman Sachs iOS      positive    755 0.576 
## 17 Wells Fargo             Android  negative    198 0.381 
## 18 Wells Fargo             Android  positive    322 0.619 
## 19 Wells Fargo             iOS      negative    229 0.377 
## 20 Wells Fargo             iOS      positive    378 0.623
### Review sentiment distribution

#To compute review level Bing sentiment, 
#the sentiment score will be calculate as positive-negative.

bing_review <- bing_words %>%
  count(bank, platform, review_id, sentiment) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = sentiment,
    values_from = n,
    values_fill = 0
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    sentiment_score = positive - negative
  )
df_sent <- df_english %>%
  left_join(bing_review, by = c("bank", "platform", "review_id"))
summary(df_sent$sentiment_score)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.    NA's 
## -14.000  -1.000   1.000   0.225   2.000  12.000     928
df_sent_plot <- df_sent %>%
  filter(is.finite(sentiment_score))

ggplot(df_sent_plot, aes(x = sentiment_score)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)),bins = 30, fill = "lightblue", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_density(color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of review-level sentiment",
    x = "Bing score = positive - negative",
    y = "Density"
  )

# sentiment score and star ratings
ggplot(df_sent_plot, aes(x = factor(rating), y = sentiment_score)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightblue", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Review sentiment by star rating",
    x = "Star rating",
    y = "Bing sentiment score"
  )

AFINN

afinn <- get_sentiments("afinn")

afinn_words <- reviews_after_sw %>%
  inner_join(afinn, by = "word")


nrow(afinn_words)
## [1] 6543
nrow(bing_words)
## [1] 8001
top_afinn_words <- afinn_words %>%
    count(word, value, sort = TRUE) %>%
    slice_head(n = 15)

top_afinn_words
## # A tibble: 15 × 3
##    word      value     n
##    <chr>     <dbl> <int>
##  1 easy          1   941
##  2 love          3   352
##  3 pay          -1   199
##  4 friendly      2   125
##  5 worst        -3   124
##  6 terrible     -3    94
##  7 bad          -3    90
##  8 support       2    89
##  9 helpful       2    82
## 10 horrible     -3    80
## 11 excellent     3    79
## 12 awesome       4    75
## 13 recommend     2    71
## 14 fine          2    69
## 15 fraud        -4    62
ggplot(top_afinn_words, aes(x = reorder(word, n), y = n)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Most frequent AFINN-scored words",
    x = "",
    y = "Frequency"
  )

# create the total AFINN score for each review and the mean of it
afinn_review <- afinn_words %>%
  group_by(bank, platform, review_id) %>%
  summarise(
    afinn_sum = sum(value),
    afinn_mean = mean(value),
    n_afinn = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

df_afinn <- df_english %>%
  mutate(rating = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(rating))) %>%
  left_join(afinn_review, by = c("bank", "platform", "review_id"))

# how many reviews received AFINN score
df_afinn %>%
  summarise(
    total_reviews = n(),
    scored_reviews = sum(!is.na(afinn_sum)),
    pct_scored = 100 * mean(!is.na(afinn_sum))
  )
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##   total_reviews scored_reviews pct_scored
##           <int>          <int>      <dbl>
## 1          4213           2993       71.0
df_afinn_plot <- df_afinn %>%
  filter(is.finite(afinn_sum))

ggplot(df_afinn_plot, aes(x = afinn_sum)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 30,
                 fill = "lightblue", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_density(color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of review-level sentiment (AFINN sum)",
    x = "AFINN sum score per review",
    y = "Density"
  )

# how tone varies by bank
afinn_bank_platform <- df_afinn %>%
  group_by(bank, platform) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_sum = mean(afinn_sum, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_mean = mean(afinn_mean, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_reviews = n(),
    n_scored = sum(!is.na(afinn_sum)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

afinn_bank_platform
## # A tibble: 10 × 6
##    bank                    platform mean_sum mean_mean n_reviews n_scored
##    <chr>                   <chr>       <dbl>     <dbl>     <int>    <int>
##  1 Bank Of America         Android     1.71     1.18         418      282
##  2 Bank Of America         iOS        -0.731   -0.0955       422      320
##  3 Chase                   Android     0.626    0.587        446      243
##  4 Chase                   iOS        -0.554   -0.0191       391      294
##  5 Citi                    Android     1.82     1.22         460      361
##  6 Citi                    iOS        -2.25    -0.796        443      336
##  7 Marcus By Goldman Sachs Android     1.11     0.721        485      338
##  8 Marcus By Goldman Sachs iOS         1.07     0.516        463      373
##  9 Wells Fargo             Android     0.792    0.671        406      231
## 10 Wells Fargo             iOS         1.14     0.752        279      215
ggplot(afinn_bank_platform, aes(x = reorder(bank, mean_sum), y = mean_sum, fill = platform)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Average AFINN sentiment by bank and platform",
    x = "",
    y = "Mean AFINN sum score"
  )

top_afinn_by_score <- afinn_words %>%
  count(value, word, sort = TRUE) %>%
  group_by(value) %>%
  slice_max(n, n = 10, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(value, desc(n))

top_afinn_by_score
## # A tibble: 79 × 3
##    value word           n
##    <dbl> <chr>      <int>
##  1    -4 fraud         62
##  2    -4 fraudulent     9
##  3    -4 hell           3
##  4    -4 damn           2
##  5    -4 wtf            2
##  6    -4 dick           1
##  7    -3 worst        124
##  8    -3 terrible      94
##  9    -3 bad           90
## 10    -3 horrible      80
## # ℹ 69 more rows
ggplot(top_afinn_by_score,
       aes(x = reorder(word, n), y = n)) +
  geom_col(fill = "lightblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  facet_wrap(~ value, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Top 10 AFINN sentiment words by score",
    subtitle = "AFINN scores range from −5 (strongly negative) to +5 (strongly positive)",
    x = "",
    y = "Frequency"
  )

df_afinn_plot <- df_afinn %>%
  filter(!is.na(rating), is.finite(afinn_sum))

ggplot(df_afinn_plot, aes(x = factor(rating), y = afinn_sum)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightblue", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "AFINN sentiment by star rating",
    x = "Star rating",
    y = "AFINN sum score"
  )

cor.test(df_afinn_plot$rating, df_afinn_plot$afinn_sum, use = "complete.obs")
## 
##  Pearson's product-moment correlation
## 
## data:  df_afinn_plot$rating and df_afinn_plot$afinn_sum
## t = 40.922, df = 2991, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.5756312 0.6215897
## sample estimates:
##       cor 
## 0.5991037

NRC

nrc <- get_sentiments("nrc")  

nrc_words <- reviews_after_sw %>%
  inner_join(nrc, by = "word")
## Warning in inner_join(., nrc, by = "word"): Detected an unexpected many-to-many relationship between `x` and `y`.
## ℹ Row 1 of `x` matches multiple rows in `y`.
## ℹ Row 6253 of `y` matches multiple rows in `x`.
## ℹ If a many-to-many relationship is expected, set `relationship =
##   "many-to-many"` to silence this warning.
glimpse(nrc_words)
## Rows: 24,685
## Columns: 19
## $ platform         <chr> "iOS", "iOS", "iOS", "iOS", "iOS", "iOS", "iOS", "iOS…
## $ storefront       <chr> "us", "us", "us", "us", "us", "us", "us", "us", "us",…
## $ app_id           <chr> "284847138.0", "284847138.0", "284847138.0", "2848471…
## $ review_id        <chr> "13566342702", "13566342702", "13566342702", "1356634…
## $ date             <dttm> 2025-12-28 20:28:49, 2025-12-28 20:28:49, 2025-12-28…
## $ user             <chr> "EBP1940", "EBP1940", "EBP1940", "EBP1940", "EBP1940"…
## $ rating           <dbl> 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 5, 5, 5, 5, 5, 5, 5, 5, 5,…
## $ title            <chr> "Horrible.", "Horrible.", "Horrible.", "Horrible.", "…
## $ review           <chr> "I can’t load the banking app bc it requires a differ…
## $ version          <chr> "25.04.06", "25.04.06", "25.04.06", "25.04.06", "25.0…
## $ package          <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, N…
## $ thumbsUpCount    <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, N…
## $ appVersion       <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, N…
## $ source_file      <chr> "bank_of_america__combined_us.csv", "bank_of_america_…
## $ raw_length_words <int> 36, 36, 36, 36, 36, 36, 36, 36, 36, 30, 30, 43, 43, 4…
## $ bank             <chr> "Bank Of America", "Bank Of America", "Bank Of Americ…
## $ language         <chr> "en", "en", "en", "en", "en", "en", "en", "en", "en",…
## $ word             <chr> "horrible", "horrible", "horrible", "horrible", "horr…
## $ sentiment        <chr> "anger", "disgust", "fear", "negative", "anger", "dis…
nrc_words %>% count(sentiment, sort = TRUE)
## # A tibble: 10 × 2
##    sentiment        n
##    <chr>        <int>
##  1 positive      5809
##  2 trust         4417
##  3 negative      3233
##  4 anticipation  2577
##  5 joy           2347
##  6 anger         1646
##  7 sadness       1427
##  8 fear          1358
##  9 surprise      1004
## 10 disgust        867
tibble(
  total_tokens = nrow(reviews_after_sw),
  nrc_tokens = nrow(nrc_words),
  pct_covered = 100 * nrow(nrc_words) / nrow(reviews_after_sw)
)
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##   total_tokens nrc_tokens pct_covered
##          <int>      <int>       <dbl>
## 1        41956      24685        58.8
nrc_words %>%
  count(sentiment, sort = TRUE) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(sentiment, n), y = n, fill = sentiment)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of NRC emotion categories",
    x = "",
    y = "Number of tokens"
  )

# NRC emotion by banks
nrc_by_bank <- nrc_words %>%
    count(bank, sentiment) %>%
    group_by(bank) %>%
    mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
    ungroup()

nrc_by_bank
## # A tibble: 50 × 4
##    bank            sentiment        n    pct
##    <chr>           <chr>        <int>  <dbl>
##  1 Bank Of America anger          268 0.0591
##  2 Bank Of America anticipation   494 0.109 
##  3 Bank Of America disgust        154 0.0340
##  4 Bank Of America fear           279 0.0615
##  5 Bank Of America joy            466 0.103 
##  6 Bank Of America negative       554 0.122 
##  7 Bank Of America positive      1073 0.237 
##  8 Bank Of America sadness        290 0.0640
##  9 Bank Of America surprise       173 0.0382
## 10 Bank Of America trust          782 0.173 
## # ℹ 40 more rows
ggplot(nrc_by_bank, aes(x = sentiment, y = pct, fill = sentiment)) +
    geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
    facet_wrap(~ bank) +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(
        title = "Emotion composition by bank (NRC)",
        x = "",
        y = "Proportion of emotion words"
    )

# nrc by platform
nrc_by_platform <- nrc_words %>%
  count(platform, sentiment) %>%
  group_by(platform) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(nrc_by_platform, aes(x = sentiment, y = pct, fill = sentiment)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ platform) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Emotion composition by platform (NRC)",
    x = "",
    y = "Proportion of emotion words"
  )

5. Bigrams

bigrams <- df_english %>%
  mutate(text_bigram = stringr::str_to_lower(raw_text),
         text_bigram = stringr::str_replace_all(text_bigram, "[^a-z\\s']", " "),
         text_bigram = stringr::str_squish(text_bigram)) %>%
  unnest_tokens(bigram, text_bigram, token = "ngrams", n = 2) %>%
  separate(bigram, into = c("word1", "word2"), sep = " ", extra = "drop")%>%
  filter(!is.na(word1), !is.na(word2))

head(bigrams)
## # A tibble: 6 × 20
##   platform storefront app_id    review_id date                user  rating title
##   <chr>    <chr>      <chr>     <chr>     <dttm>              <chr>  <dbl> <chr>
## 1 iOS      us         28484713… 13566342… 2025-12-28 20:28:49 EBP1…      1 Horr…
## 2 iOS      us         28484713… 13566342… 2025-12-28 20:28:49 EBP1…      1 Horr…
## 3 iOS      us         28484713… 13566342… 2025-12-28 20:28:49 EBP1…      1 Horr…
## 4 iOS      us         28484713… 13566342… 2025-12-28 20:28:49 EBP1…      1 Horr…
## 5 iOS      us         28484713… 13566342… 2025-12-28 20:28:49 EBP1…      1 Horr…
## 6 iOS      us         28484713… 13566342… 2025-12-28 20:28:49 EBP1…      1 Horr…
## # ℹ 12 more variables: review <chr>, version <chr>, package <chr>,
## #   thumbsUpCount <chr>, appVersion <chr>, source_file <chr>, raw_text <chr>,
## #   raw_length_words <int>, bank <chr>, language <chr>, word1 <chr>,
## #   word2 <chr>
negators <- c(
  "no", "not", "nor", "never", "none", "neither", "cannot",
  "hardly", "rarely", "barely", "n't", "dont", "don't", "isn't", "wasn't", "aren't", "won't",
  "shouldn't", "couldn't", "wouldn't", "doesn't", "didn't",
  "haven't", "hasn't", "hadn't", "can't", "cant")

custom_stop_words <- tidytext::stop_words %>%
  filter(!word %in% negators) %>%
  bind_rows(
    tibble(
      word = c("app", "bank", "banking", "mobile",
               "iphone", "apple", "android", "phone"),
      lexicon = "custom"
    )
  )

bigrams <- bigrams %>%
  filter(!word1 %in% custom_stop_words$word,
         !word2 %in% custom_stop_words$word)


bigram_counts <- bigrams %>%
  count(word1, word2, sort = TRUE)

head(bigram_counts, 20)
## # A tibble: 20 × 3
##    word1    word2        n
##    <chr>    <chr>    <int>
##  1 customer service    235
##  2 credit   card       153
##  3 user     friendly    83
##  4 savings  account     62
##  5 credit   cards       42
##  6 dark     mode        35
##  7 checking account     28
##  8 marcus   insights    27
##  9 multiple times       27
## 10 customer support     25
## 11 goldman  sachs       24
## 12 external accounts    23
## 13 marcus   account     22
## 14 no       issues      22
## 15 send     money       22
## 16 blank    screen      21
## 17 debit    card        21
## 18 no       reason      21
## 19 bill     pay         19
## 20 deposit  checks      19
top20_bigrams <- bigram_counts %>%
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%
  mutate(bigram = paste(word1, word2))

ggplot(top20_bigrams, aes(x = reorder(bigram, n), y = n)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Top 20 Most Frequent Bigrams in Mobile Banking Reviews",
    x = "",
    y = "Frequency"
  )

6. Quantify Negation Effect

negated_afinn <- bigrams %>%
  filter(word1 %in% negators) %>%
  inner_join(afinn, by = c("word2" = "word")) %>%
  mutate(
    negated_value = -value
  )

negation_by_bigram <- negated_afinn %>%
  group_by(word1, word2) %>%
  summarise(
    original_value = mean(value),
    negated_value = mean(negated_value),
    n_occurrences = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

negation_by_bigram
## # A tibble: 98 × 5
##    word1   word2    original_value negated_value n_occurrences
##    <chr>   <chr>             <dbl>         <dbl>         <int>
##  1 barely  worth                 2            -2             1
##  2 can't   complain             -2             2             1
##  3 can't   pay                  -1             1             1
##  4 can't   trust                 1            -1             1
##  5 cannot  cancel               -1             1             1
##  6 cannot  restore               1            -1             1
##  7 didn't  care                  2            -2             1
##  8 doesn't crash                -2             2             2
##  9 doesn't fit                   1            -1             1
## 10 doesn't lag                  -1             1             1
## # ℹ 88 more rows
# how much distortion would cost from negators per star rating?
neg_by_rating <- negated_afinn %>%
  group_by(rating) %>%
  summarise (
    total_negation_shift = sum(negated_value),
    mean_negation_shift = mean(negated_value),
    n_negated_bigrams = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
reviews_per_rating <- df_english %>%
  count(rating, name = "n_reviews")

negation_by_rating_norm <- neg_by_rating %>%
  left_join(reviews_per_rating, by = "rating") %>%
  mutate(
    negation_shift_per_review = total_negation_shift / n_reviews,
    negated_bigrams_per_review = n_negated_bigrams / n_reviews
  )

negation_by_rating_norm
## # A tibble: 5 × 7
##   rating total_negation_shift mean_negation_shift n_negated_bigrams n_reviews
##    <dbl>                <dbl>               <dbl>             <int>     <int>
## 1      1                 -151              -1.23                123      1410
## 2      2                  -30              -1.25                 24       260
## 3      3                  -13              -1.3                  10       214
## 4      4                    2               0.286                 7       268
## 5      5                   32               1.23                 26      2061
## # ℹ 2 more variables: negation_shift_per_review <dbl>,
## #   negated_bigrams_per_review <dbl>
ggplot(negation_by_rating_norm,
       aes(x = factor(rating), y = negation_shift_per_review)) +
  geom_col(fill = "firebrick") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Average negation-induced sentiment correction by star rating",
    x = "Star rating",
    y = "Negation adjustment per review (AFINN)"
  )

7. Compare unigram vs bigram with negators

# classic afinn per review (unigram)
afinn_classic <- reviews_after_sw %>%
  inner_join(get_sentiments("afinn"), by = "word") %>%
  group_by(review_id, bank, platform, rating) %>%
  summarise(
    afinn_sum = sum(value),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# bigram with negators
negation_review <- negated_afinn %>%
  group_by(review_id) %>%
  summarise(
    negated_sum = sum(negated_value),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# combine to compare
sentiment_compare <- afinn_classic %>%
  left_join(negation_review, by = "review_id") %>%
  mutate(
    negated_sum = replace_na(negated_sum, 0),
    afinn_negation = afinn_sum + negated_sum
  )
sentiment_compare <- sentiment_compare %>%
  mutate(
    sentiment_diff = afinn_negation - afinn_sum
  )

afinn_sentiment_stars <- sentiment_compare %>%
  group_by(rating) %>%
  summarise(
    overall_sentiment = mean(afinn_sum, na.rm = TRUE),
    overall_sentiment_negaware = mean(afinn_negation, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_reviews = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(Stars = factor(rating, levels = sort(unique(rating))))

afinn_sentiment_comparison <- afinn_sentiment_stars %>%
  select(Stars, overall_sentiment, overall_sentiment_negaware) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("overall_sentiment"),
    names_to = "type",
    values_to = "score"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    type = recode(type,
                  "overall_sentiment" = "Classic (no negation)",
                  "overall_sentiment_negaware" = "Negation-aware"),
    Stars = forcats::fct_reorder(Stars, score, .fun = mean)
  )

ggplot(afinn_sentiment_comparison,
       aes(x = Stars, y = score, fill = type)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Paired") +
  labs(
    title = "Overall Sentiment by Star Rating: comparison",
    subtitle = "AFINN lexicon (classic vs negation-aware bigram adjustment)",
    x = "Stars",
    y = "Mean AFINN sentiment score",
    fill = "Method"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

8. Evaluation

In this part, statistical test will be perform to check whether there are difference in reviews between platforms and whether negation-aware actually improve alignment with star ratings. The tests will be calculate using AFINN score per review.

# platform comparison
wilcox.test(
  afinn_sum ~ platform,
  data = df_afinn
)
## 
##  Wilcoxon rank sum test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  afinn_sum by platform
## W = 1355614, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true location shift is not equal to 0
# correlation comparison (classic vs negation-aware)
cor_classic <- cor(
  sentiment_compare$rating,
  sentiment_compare$afinn_sum,
  use = "complete.obs"
)

cor_negaware <- cor(
  sentiment_compare$rating,
  sentiment_compare$afinn_negation,
  use = "complete.obs"
)

tibble(
  method = c("Classic AFINN", "Negation-aware AFINN"),
  correlation_with_rating = c(cor_classic, cor_negaware)
)
## # A tibble: 2 × 2
##   method               correlation_with_rating
##   <chr>                                  <dbl>
## 1 Classic AFINN                          0.599
## 2 Negation-aware AFINN                   0.615
